user_query:
I need a ready-to-open eNSP topology file for a small campus edge. Read input/requirements.md for context and acceptance criteria, then generate a valid eNSP .topo XML and save it to output/campus-network.xml (use the latest version string). Use these exact devices, names, and models — do not add extras:
- R1 (AR2220)
- R2 (AR2220)
- FW1 (USG6000V)
- SW1 (S5700)
- SW2 (S5700)
- PC-A (PC)
- PC-B (PC)
- Server1 (Server)
- Internet (Cloud)
- AC1 (AC6005)
- AP1 (AP6050)
Connections (use the correct lineName for each):
- Serial: R1 <-> R2
- Copper: R1 <-> FW1, R2 <-> FW1, FW1 <-> SW1, FW1 <-> SW2, SW1 <-> PC-A, SW2 <-> PC-B, SW2 <-> Server1, SW1 <-> AC1, SW1 <-> AP1, R1 <-> Internet
Layout and annotations:
- Use a grid-based auto layout (start ~100,100; ~200 px horizontal, ~150 px vertical spacing). Group logically so core devices are together and access devices together.
- Add at least two rectangular area boxes (type=1 shapes): one blue (color=255) that contains the core (R1, R2, FW1, SW1, SW2), and one yellow (color=16776960) that contains the access devices (PC-A, PC-B, Server1, AC1, AP1).
- Add at least three text labels (txttips) with these exact contents somewhere readable: "VLAN10 - Users", "VLAN20 - Servers", "Serial link R1-R2 (DCE/DTE)".
Technical requirements:
- Root element: <topo version="1.3.00.100"> with devices, lines, shapes, and txttips sections.
- Every device must have a valid UUID v4 in the id attribute, a model matching the list above, and reasonable cx/cy plus edit_left/edit_top values.
- Lines must reference devices by UUIDs, and the interfacePair must have lineName set to Serial or Copper as specified.
- Keep the devices section to exactly these 11 devices and the lines section to exactly the 11 connections listed above.
Write a single XML file to output/campus-network.xml. Do not place files anywhere else or use absolute paths.
